children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The term “applicable premium” means, with respect to any period of continuation coverage of qualified beneficiaries, the cost to the plan for such period of the coverage for similarly situated beneficiaries with respect to whom a qualifying event has not occurred (without regard to whether such cost is paid by the employer or employee).

children.label: i
children.text: is determined on an actuarial basis, and

children.label: ii
children.text: takes into account such factors as the Secretary may prescribe in regulations.

children.heading: Determination on basis of past cost
children.label: B Determination on basis of past cost
i
children.text: the cost to the plan for similarly situated beneficiaries for the same period occurring during the preceding determination period under paragraph (3), adjusted by

children.label: ii
children.text: the percentage increase or decrease in the implicit price deflator of the gross national product (calculated by the Department of Commerce and published in the Survey of Current Business) for the 12-month period ending on the last day of the sixth month of such preceding determination period.

children.heading: Subparagraph (B) not to apply where significant change
children.label: C Subparagraph (B) not to apply where significant change
children.text: An administrator may not elect to have subparagraph (B) apply in any case in which there is any significant difference, between the determination period and the preceding determination period, in coverage under, or in employees covered by, the plan. The determination under the preceding sentence for any determination period shall be made at the same time as the determination under paragraph (3).

children.heading: Determination period
children.label: 3 Determination period
children.text: The determination of any applicable premium shall be made for a period of 12 months and shall be made before the beginning of such period.
